_note: CONSTRUCTED evaluation scenario for H7 (defeasible reconciliation). State/local claims are illustrative fixtures written in the style of the cited manuals. This directory is intentionally separate from seed_data/rules/ and is never seeded into, or counted toward, the verified rule corpus.
description: State DOT standard narrows the federal lane-width default on the state trunk network: AASHTO allows 11-12 ft for rural arterials, WisDOT requires the full 12-ft lane. Demonstrates a jurisdiction-priority defeat (state over federal) and a non-monotonic verdict flip: an 11-ft lane is compliant under federal rules alone, noncompliant once the state rule enters.

claims.name: Rural Arterial Lane Width (AASHTO)
claims.citation: AASHTO Green Book, 7th Ed., Ch. 7 (Rural and Urban Arterials)
claims.description: National default: rural arterial travel lanes 11-12 ft.

claims.name: State Trunk Highway Standard Lane (WisDOT, constructed)
claims.description: State standard: 12-ft lanes required on the state trunk network.
